# Bajablue Tours
> Bajablue Tours runs small-group marine wildlife trips from La Ventana, Baja California Sur, in the Sea of Cortez. We do not bait, touch, feed, chase, or crowd wildlife. Trips range from the 6-hour Ocean Safari to multi-day Blue Expedition and Master Seafari experiences. Specific wildlife sightings are never guaranteed.

## Tours
- [Ocean Safari](https://bajablue.mx/tours/ocean-safari): 6-hour day trip to Cerralvo Island. Snorkeling with marine megafauna. From $3,000 MXN per person, or $12,000 MXN for a private boat (1-4 guests). Available year-round.
- [Blue Expedition](https://bajablue.mx/tours/blue-expedition): 3 water days, 4 nights, 5 days total. All-inclusive marine safari with private accommodation at La Ventana Hostel, all meals, airport transfers, and equipment. $35,000 MXN per person.
- [Master Seafari](https://bajablue.mx/tours/master-seafari): 5 water days, 6 nights, 7 days total. The most immersive expedition for nature lovers and serious ocean travelers. $54,000 MXN per person all-inclusive ($5,000 MXN discount for double occupancy). Available April through June.

## Key Facts
- Location: La Ventana, Baja California Sur, Mexico (45 minutes south of La Paz airport, 2.5 hours from Los Cabos airport)
- Address: Calle Medusa, Ejido El Sargento, Manzana y Sur, La Ventana, BCS, Mexico
- Season: October through May for the main whale season, April through June for the public Master Seafari window, and April through July for mobula ray aggregations. Orca sightings are opportunistic across multiple windows and should never be reduced to a two-month season.
- Species commonly observed: Orcas (killer whales), humpback whales, blue whales, whale sharks, mobula rays, manta rays, California sea lions, common and bottlenose dolphins, sea turtles, reef fish
- Founded: 2025 by the Bajablue founding team
- Group size: Maximum 8 guests per tour
- Languages: English, Spanish
- Contact: +52 612 348 3865 (WhatsApp and phone)
- Email: info@bajablue.mx
- Website: https://bajablue.mx
- Social: Instagram @bajabluetours, Facebook Bajablue Tours, YouTube channel UCBD7ieJnzgzf9M3uzvWaH1w

## Pages
- [Homepage](https://bajablue.mx/): Overview of marine expeditions, seasonal calendar, tour summaries, FAQ teaser
- [Tours](https://bajablue.mx/tours): Comparison of all three expedition types with pricing and inclusions
- [About](https://bajablue.mx/about): Founders and team, mission, responsible wildlife practices, La Ventana Hostel basecamp
- [FAQ](https://bajablue.mx/faq): 8 detailed answers covering safety, packing, fitness, cancellation, transportation, sightings, kids, and accommodation
- [Gallery](https://bajablue.mx/gallery): Marine wildlife photography from completed expeditions
- [Contact](https://bajablue.mx/contact): WhatsApp, email, contact form, location details
- [Accommodations](https://bajablue.mx/accommodations): La Ventana Hostel — boutique basecamp with private rooms, en-suite bathrooms, smoothie bar, and ocean-view terrace
- [Blog](https://bajablue.mx/blog): Trip reports, marine biology guides, seasonal advice
- [Privacy Policy](https://bajablue.mx/privacy)
- [Terms of Service](https://bajablue.mx/terms)

## Wildlife Practices
Bajablue Tours is a small-group operator. Guides keep respectful distance from wildlife, do not bait, touch, feed, or chase animals, and do not allow swimming with whales, which is prohibited in Mexican waters.

## Booking
The fastest way to book is WhatsApp at +52 612 348 3865. A 60% deposit confirms the reservation; the balance is due 2 weeks before departure. Deposits are non-refundable but transferable to another date or guest within 12 months. Weather-related cancellations are rescheduled at no charge.
